input.background: Suppose there are 500 items in the lot and their manufacturer claims that no more than 5% are defective. When the shipment is received at an assembly plant, a quality control manager asks his staff to take a random sample of size 20 from it without replacement. The manager will accept the lot if there are at most 1 defective item in the sample. If 5% of the items are defective in the entire lot, then it consists of 25 defective items.
input.question: What is the probability distribution of the number of defective items in a sample of size 20?

output.answer: Let X be the number of defective items in a sample of size 20, then it has a hypergeometric distribution and its probability function is as follows: $$ P(X=x)=\frac{\binom{25}{x}\binom{475}{20-x}}{\binom{500}{20}} \quad x=0,1,2, \ldots, 20 $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
